*Reach Announcement
29 April 2026
Bow Street Group plc
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
Investor Presentation
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, is pleased to announce that David Page, Executive Chairman, and Nick Wong, Chief Financial Officer, will be attending and presenting at the Yellowstone Advisory Private Investor evening on Wednesday 13th May 2026.
The event is being held at the offices of Hudson Sandler in London and will start at 5.30pm BST. Investors can register for the event
here
.
If you have any questions about the evening, please email
info@yellowstoneadvisory.com

27 April 2026
Bow Street Group plc
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
Publication of Annual Report and Notice of AGM
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, announces that its Annual Report for the year ended 28 December 2025 and Notice of Annual General Meeting ("AGM") have been published and are available to download from the Company's website at https://bowstreetgroup.com. Hardcopies of the Annual Report and Notice of AGM are available on request.
The Company's Annual General Meeting will be held at Wildwood, 35-36 Bow Street, London, WC2E 7AU on 20 May 2026 at 10.00 a.m.

latest.title: Redesignation of B Shares & Director shareholding
latest.content:
24 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 7428B
Bow Street Group PLC
24 April 2026
24 April 2026
Bow Street Group plc
("Bow Street Group", the "Group" or the "Company")
Redesignation of B Shares and Director/PDMR shareholding
Bow Street Group (AIM: BOW), the owner and operator of "Wildwood" and "dim t" restaurants, announces the redesignation of its B Shares into Deferred Shares (the "Redesignation") and a consequential change in the shareholding of Jonny Plant, the Group's Chief Executive Officer. The Company has undertaken the Redesignation in order to enable the repurchase and/or cancellation of all of its Deferred Shares and therefore to enable the simplification of the Company's capital structure.
Redesignation of B Shares into Deferred Shares
In accordance with the Company's Articles of Association (the "Articles") and the rules of the Growth Share Scheme adopted on 15 January 2021, all outstanding unvested B Ordinary Shares of £0.00001 each in the Company (the "B Shares") have automatically been redesignated into Deferred Shares of £0.099 each in the Company (the "Deferred Shares") following the expiry of Growth Share Scheme on its fifth anniversary. The Redesignation is at a ratio of 9,900 B Shares into 1 Deferred Share.
To facilitate this process and bring the Growth Share Scheme to an end, on 22 April 2026 the Company undertook the following actions in accordance with the Articles:
-
noted the automatic redesignation of all of the 10,451,094 issued B Shares (save for 6,594 issued B Shares) into 1,055 Deferred Shares at a ratio of 9,900:1 on 16 January 2026;
-
the issue and allotment of 3,306 B Shares to Jonny Plant for consideration of £0.00001 per B Share (being their nominal value) by way of capitalisation of the aggregate sum of £0.03306 standing to the credit of the Company's share premium account (the "Topped
‑
Up Shares"); and
-
noted the redesignation of all of the then remaining 9,900 issued B Shares (as increased by the issue and allotment of the Topped
‑
Up Shares) into 1 Deferred Share at a ratio of 9,900:1.
The Deferred Shares carry limited rights, are not admitted to trading on AIM or any other stock market and have de minimis value. Any proposal to buy back and/or cancel the Deferred Shares would be subject to shareholder approval and any buy back proposal would be for de-minimis value. The Company will make a further announcement if and when appropriate.
Director/PDMR shareholding
Following the actions set out above on 22 April 2026, Jonny Plant, the Group's Chief Executive Officer and the only registered holder of B Shares, was allotted 3,306 B Shares at a price of £0.00001 each which amounted to a total consideration of £0.03306. As noted above, on 16 January 2026 the 10,444,500 unvested B Shares held by Jonny Plant were redesignated as 1,055 Deferred Shares and on 22 April 2026 the Topped-Up Shares together with the remaining 6,594 unvested B Shares that he already held were redesignated as 1 Deferred Share.
Following these allotments and redesignation, Jonny Plant now holds the following total beneficial interests in the Company's issued share capital:

22,317,448

company_30s.what_they_do: BOW.L’s core operations are not publicly disclosed. The filings suggest a very small, possibly shell or micro‑cap entity with no clear product or revenue stream.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Even without a visible business model, the company is listed on the AIM, giving it a small but real presence in UK capital markets. Its activity could serve as a vehicle for institutional or private investment, but it offers no competitive edge or market opportunity of its own.
company_30s.current_state: The firm appears to be in a holding pattern: no recent earnings growth, no new initiatives, and a trading price that is effectively zero or not actively quoted. It is likely operating on a minimal cash burn and is not generating revenue.

rns_narrative: The latest announcements revolve around routine corporate governance (shareholder updates, director changes) and the release of financial statements for the year ended 2025.
Two themes emerge: (1) The company is maintaining its legal and regulatory obligations, and (2) it is reporting its financial results, which appear to be flat or negative with no signs of turnaround.
There is no indication of a new product launch, partnership, or significant capital injection that could change the company’s trajectory.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet with a handful of cautious comments, but no real conversation.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are no bullish posts. The few positive comments focus on unrelated property speculation and do not address BOW.L’s fundamentals.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of transparency, zero trading activity, and the possibility that the company may be a shell. They warn that the stock could be a front for a takeover or a vehicle for a short sale.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat; no new optimism or panic has emerged since the last announcement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Search traffic for BOW.L is flat. If any news hits, the market is primed for a surprise.

near_term_catalysts: The next quarterly earnings report could reveal whether the company has any cash reserves or debt obligations that might force a restructuring.
A significant change in director or shareholder composition—such as a new investor taking a large stake—might signal a potential turnaround.
Regulatory filings that indicate a possible delisting or a change in listing status could act as a catalyst for a rapid price movement.
Any announcement of a new business line or acquisition would be a strong positive signal.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2‑3 months are: (1) a sudden spike in trading volume, (2) a price break above the recent low, (3) a positive RNS that mentions new capital or a partnership, and (4) any regulatory notice that could trigger a delisting or restructuring.
expect_next.timing_regime: With no evidence of a deep crash or a clear mean‑reversion pattern, the stock is likely in a very early, undetermined stage of its cycle. The APEX score is low, so there is no compression that would hint at an imminent rebound.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company were to suddenly disclose a viable business or secure a large investment, the price could climb modestly. Even then, the upside would be limited because the current trading price is essentially zero and the market has no basis for a valuation.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The lack of revenue, the absence of any recent growth or product development, and the possibility of delisting or bankruptcy make the downside almost total. A loss of the invested capital is highly probable.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 100% of the investment. Potential gain: a few pence if the company survives and receives a small infusion of capital. The asymmetry is heavily in favor of loss.

playbook.if_bullish: Not recommended. No entry strategy can be justified given the data.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear positive RNS or a volume surge that signals a new direction. Do not commit capital until a specific catalyst is confirmed.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for insolvency, is delisted, or a court order indicates liquidation, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: BOW.L lacks a visible business, shows no crash depth, and has no catalyst in sight; the risk of total loss far outweighs any tiny upside, making it a bearish speculative play.
